experiences.intentions: The board is empty and the active I piece is horizontal near the top, spanning columns 3-6. With no existing stack or threats, the best immediate sub-goal is efficient placement to set up future flat stacking. Candidate moves: left/right only shift the piece without locking; rotate would make it vertical, less ideal on an empty board; soft_drop advances only one row. Hard dropping the horizontal I to the bottom creates a flat 4-cell segment on the floor and quickly progresses the game state without introducing holes.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is almost empty except for a horizontal I piece locked on the bottom row spanning columns 4-7, leaving a flat low stack. The active T is near the top in spawn orientation. Goal now is strong early placement that preserves a flat surface and sets up future clean stacking with upcoming O,S,J,Z. Dropping the T immediately in its current orientation would create a central bump on the I. Rotating to a flatter-support orientation is possible later, but first shifting horizontally is more flexible. Moving left starts guiding the T toward the left side where it can be placed with less interference and keeps options open for later rotation/drop.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is mostly empty except for a horizontal I on the bottom row spanning columns 3-6 and the active T near the top in its spawn orientation. With an empty field, the main opportunity is to place the T efficiently to build a flat surface for upcoming O,S,J,Z pieces. Dropping immediately in the current center position would create a small bump above the existing I rather than tucking usefully. A lateral move is the best setup step; moving left starts shifting the T toward the side where it can be dropped to complement the bottom I and keep the center cleaner. Rotation is not urgent because all orientations remain available later, and hard/soft drop now commits too early.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for a settled I piece lying horizontally on the bottom row columns 3-6, and the active T is high near the left-center in its flat orientation. With no immediate threats and lots of space, the goal is to place pieces efficiently to build a flat stack and preserve flexibility for upcoming O,S,J,Z. Dropping the current T immediately in its present column would create a bump above the existing I. Moving horizontally first is better; shifting right can help place the T adjacent to or over a cleaner area before committing. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop commit too early in a suboptimal spot, rotations are not yet necessary on an open board, and no_op wastes a turn. Moving right advances toward a cleaner placement plan.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Board is mostly empty with a flat I-piece floor at the bottom spanning columns 3-6, and the active T is high above near center in its spawn orientation. Goal is to place the T efficiently without creating holes; the best immediate sub-goal is to move toward a clean fit over the 4-wide platform. Rotating now is unnecessary on an empty board, and dropping immediately in the current column could miss better alignment options. Shifting left starts positioning the T to stack neatly on the left side of the I bar, preserving flexibility for the upcoming O, S, J, Z pieces.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top center, with open space below and a somewhat uneven stack concentrated on the lower left/middle. Immediate threat is low since the board is mostly empty. Goal is to place the Z efficiently to build a flatter surface and avoid creating holes for upcoming L,J,O,T pieces. Candidate actions: moving left could aim the Z toward the left side contours; moving right drifts toward a flatter but less connected area; rotating vertical is premature without horizontal alignment; dropping now would commit too early. Best sub-goal now is to start shifting the Z left toward the central-left surface where it can likely rest cleanly above the SS/T area later.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is a Z near the top with plenty of air below; the stack is low and relatively flat except for minor bumps around the center-left. Goal is to place this Z efficiently to avoid creating holes and ideally complement the existing center structure. Immediate lateral movement is possible, but with only one action now, the best progress is to accelerate descent while keeping options open; the current horizontal Z can still be steered on later ticks if needed. Rotating now is not clearly better than preserving flexibility, and hard drop is premature without finer placement. So soft dropping advances toward placement safely.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high near center-left with plenty of time before lock. Surface below has a shallow left structure and a central-right stack; immediate threat is low, so best sub-goal is to improve future placement rather than drop early. Candidate moves: hard/soft drop would commit to current column prematurely; rotations change shape but not yet necessary with open air. Moving left shifts the L toward the left side where it can potentially tuck against the existing left stack on later moves, while right heads toward flatter right side but may interfere with future O/T flow. So the best single-step setup is left.

Immediate threats are low since the pile is only near the bottom. Goal is to place the L to improve surface quality for upcoming J,O,T,S pieces, ideally avoiding creating overhangs. From spawn, moving horizontally while still high preserves flexibility; hard/soft drop now would commit too early, and rotation is not yet necessary because the flat L orientation is versatile. Shifting right positions the piece toward the more open center-right where it can later slot cleanly above the existing bumps.

experiences.intentions: The active L piece is high above the stack with plenty of lateral freedom. The surface below has a central valley around columns 3-5, and the current spawn orientation already has the L nub on the right, which can fit well if guided down. Immediate goal is to position efficiently before committing; dropping now would freeze far too high and waste placement. Among candidates, moving left starts aligning the piece over the central-left gap and preserves future rotation options, while right moves away from the useful cavity and rotations are premature at this height. Soft drop also gives up horizontal setup tempo. So the best action now is to shift left.

Among options, left starts horizontal movement toward that target while preserving flexibility; rotating now is premature, dropping now would strand it high above the stack, and no_op wastes a turn.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the upper left with open air below; stack is low and the only meaningful near-term objective is to place it cleanly without creating awkward overhangs. The lower surface has some unevenness around the left-center, and a quick drop is efficient because lateral/rotation adjustments at the current height are not clearly improving over the present orientation. Candidate moves: left/right only shift within open space and postpone placement; rotations could set up different future fits but no immediate cavity is available under the piece yet. Hard dropping the current horizontal L on the left should settle it onto the existing left structure, keeping tempo and avoiding unnecessary delay.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near the center-left in rotation 0. The stack below has a broad left/middle surface with a notable vertical cavity around the center and a low overall height, so there is no immediate top-out threat. Goal is to place J efficiently to improve the surface and avoid sealing useful gaps before upcoming O,T,S,I pieces. Immediate drops from the current column would be premature while the piece is still high; shifting toward the left side sets up a cleaner tuck onto the uneven left plateau than moving right, and rotating is not yet necessary because horizontal J can still be adjusted after lateral movement. Therefore the best current step is to move left.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J near the upper-left. Surface below is much higher on the left than center/right, with a deep open well on the far right and some uneven cavities in the left stack. Immediate goal is to place the J to improve the left/mid surface without blocking future long-piece access to the right-side well. Candidate moves: hard_drop now in current orientation would likely lock awkwardly on the far left top; soft_drop just commits downward without improving alignment; rotations could make a vertical J that may fit better later, but at current spawn x the most useful placements appear slightly to the right over the left-center contours. Moving right preserves options for either a vertical tuck or flatter placement next turn while keeping the right well open. Best action now is right.

experiences.intentions: The active T piece is high near the center with a mostly flat empty field above a jagged lower stack. The immediate goal is to position it for a useful placement without committing too early. There is a visible single-cell notch around the center-left lower stack that a T could eventually help around, and the next queue starts S,I,O,J so preserving flexibility and central access is valuable. Candidate actions: hard_drop would waste the T onto a broad flat surface with little information advantage; soft_drop also commits downward before alignment. Rotations don’t yet create a clearly superior shape in the open air. Moving left/right is best to start lining up for the central-left structure; left shifts the T toward the accessible notch area while keeping future rotation options open.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active T is high near the center with lots of empty space below, so there is no immediate threat. The stack has a broad uneven surface with a deep central/right valley and no line-clear setup available from the current spawn. Goal is to improve future placement while keeping options open for upcoming S, I, O, J. Rotating now is unnecessary, and dropping immediately would commit too early from a high spawn. Shifting right moves the T toward the more open central-right area, preserving flexible placements into the valley on later moves.

experiences.intentions: The active T piece is high and unobstructed, so there is no immediate danger. The stack below has a relatively open center-left and a bumpier right side with existing overhangs; preserving flexibility matters more than rushing a drop. A good sub-goal is to steer the T toward a useful central placement for either filling unevenness or setting up future line clears/T opportunities. From the current spawn, moving left begins aligning the piece with the broader central cavity, while right moves it toward a narrower side and dropping now would commit too early. Rotation is not yet necessary because there is ample space and horizontal T is fine while repositioning. Therefore the best current action is to move left.

experiences.intentions: The active T piece is high above the stack in its spawn orientation. The surface below has a notable central-left cavity and uneven bumps, while the right side is relatively open. Immediate threats are low because the stack is only in the lower half, so this turn should focus on positioning the T for a useful tuck rather than dropping prematurely. Candidate actions: left starts moving toward the more interesting central-left structure; right moves away from the main cavity; rotations could prepare a tuck but are premature before horizontal alignment; soft/hard drop would commit too early. Best sub-goal is to shift left now to line up future placement options over the center-left gap.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current active T is near the upper-middle, resting above a jagged stack. The surface has a clear T-shaped notch around columns 2-4 near rows 17-18, but the current piece is still high and needs horizontal/rotation setup before dropping. Immediate goal is to align the T for a useful placement that preserves the stack and can fit future S,I,O,J pieces. From the shown rotations, rotation 2 is the flat-top T orientation, which is the most promising for eventually slotting into the central notch. A clockwise rotation now changes from the current up-facing T to the right-facing T, beginning that setup while there is plenty of space and no urgency. Left/right or dropping now would commit position too early; hard drop is clearly premature.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S near the top in vertical orientation. Surface below has a central T-stack around columns 4-6 and generally uneven lower structure, but there is plenty of empty space under the spawn area, so immediate threat is low. Goal is to place the S efficiently without creating overhangs; the visible rotation 0 makes the S horizontal, which is generally easier to slide into a flatter position on the left or center later. Candidate actions: left/right only shift spawn position without improving fit yet; drops commit too early from a high spawn; no_op wastes a turn. Rotating left/right changes to the horizontal S, giving more placement options before moving/downing. Choose rotate_right to reach that useful orientation now.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is S near the upper middle, resting above a T/J/O stack with open space on both sides. Surface below is uneven, with a shallow notch around columns 4-5; preserving flexibility matters since next pieces are I,O,J,L. Goal is to place the S cleanly without creating overhangs or blocking future line clears. Rotating right changes the S to a vertical-ish orientation that can better tuck into the contour near the center-right, reducing holes compared with immediate drops or lateral moves from the current horizontal orientation. Candidate drops now would likely leave a wider overhang; moving first is possible but rotation is the strongest setup step.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I horizontally at the very top with a vertical rotation available at the same x. Surface below is jagged, with a deep open well near the far left/center-left and no immediate line clear from dropping the horizontal I now. Goal is to place the I to improve stack structure and preserve future clears. Rotating vertical creates a more flexible piece that can be slid into a column/well on subsequent moves, whereas moving or dropping horizontally now is less useful and more likely to create overhangs. Best immediate sub-goal is to rotate first.

Dropping now would commit too early.
